steps.title: Follow the surplus
steps.content: The passage says river water helped Sumerians grow enough crops that they often harvested more than they needed in a season. "As a result" asks for the consequence of that surplus.

steps.title: Reject unrelated results
steps.content: Choice A invents a harvest schedule based on hot days. Choice B returns to shielding crops from sun, but the passage says river water solved the drying problem. Choice C contradicts the timeline around 4000 BCE. The remaining choice explains what a community can do with extra crops. The answer is D.
